hq: Not disclosed (no physical offices; all cloud-based)
signing_authority: Not named (placeholder 'Your Name Here' in report - signing authority name and designation left blank)

system_description.overview: Talentify is an AI-automated compliance platform designed to help companies achieve and maintain compliance with frameworks such as SOC 2, HIPAA, GDPR, ISO 27001, and PCI DSS. The platform leverages AI for evidence gathering, continuous compliance monitoring, code scanning, infrastructure monitoring, security questionnaire completion, and access control management.

products.Automated Compliance Management Platform: End-to-end compliance automation for SOC 2, HIPAA, ISO 27001, GDPR, PCI DSS with integration capabilities, continuous monitoring, gap identification, and automated evidence collection
products.AI-Powered Security and Compliance Featu: Automated code scanning for security vulnerabilities, infrastructure scanning, access control monitoring, and security questionnaire automation
products.Compliance Support Services: White-glove onboarding, implementation support, expert compliance guidance, and auditor coordination

infrastructure.cloud_provider: AWS (carve-out method - AWS controls excluded from scope)
regions: Not specifically named; network diagram shows VPC WEST and VPC EAST implying multi-region
infrastructure.physical_presence: None - no physical offices, all data processed on cloud services
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (report states 'high availability data centers with multiple availability zones'; diagram shows 3 AZs)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs). Network diagram shows VPC WEST and VPC EAST with private subnets, connected via VPC VPN Gateways and IPSec VPN tunnel.
network_architecture.api_gateway: Firewalls configured on the application gateway (mentioned in CC6.6.1)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on the application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy settings
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN. IPSec VPN tunnel between VPC regions. VPC VPN Gateways shown in network diagram.
network_architecture.tls: TLS using certificates from established third-party certificate authority for all web-based systems
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server for internet access restriction
network_architecture.topology: Internet -> VPC VPN Connection -> Internet Gateways -> VPC WEST (3 AZs with private subnets for Prod HA, Dev, Test, B.con) and VPC EAST (1 AZ with Prod HR) -> VPC VPN Gateways -> IPSec VPN Tunnel -> Sub Power, IOS, Controller (main), Controller (Backup). NOTE: Network diagram is a SAMPLE template placeholder, not actual architecture.

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments (CC8.1.4 confirms changes developed and tested in separate environment before implementation)

databases: Databases (generic, mentioned as part of infrastructure)
data_storage.backup_frequency: Not specifically stated (backup policy exists, 'suitable backups are taken and maintained')

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS Password Policy / Active Directory (mentioned in CC6.1.2)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for administrative access to production platform (CC6.1.1), for source code management tool access (CC8.1.3), and for employee email access
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination of employment
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Passwords controlled through AWS Password Policy with periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts. Local users cannot modify password rules.

encryption.key_management: Cloud provider (AWS) key management service for encryption keys at rest; access to production encryption keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool (not named; described as logging changes with timestamps and author attribution, MFA-protected access)
ci_cd_devops.ci_cd_tool: Not named (internal tool used for tracking changes from initiation through deployment and validation)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to production requires additional approval (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Infrastructure as code implied (baseline configurations retained within infrastructure as a code, configuration management tool used for rollback - CC7.1.1)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management with monthly reviews by CTO/Management. Workstations configured for automatic updates. Daily patch compliance scans on workstations. Patches tested for stability before production deployment.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (monitoring tools used to continuously monitor system capacity and performance - CC5.2.1)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not named (logging enabled to monitor administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level - CC7.2.1)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events (CC7.2.1). Predefined threshold-based alerts trigger incident management process (CC5.2.1).

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure and data center services (subservice organization)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and malware protection installed on all production servers (continuous auto-scan, weekly definition updates) and all Mac-based workstations and laptops. Email scanning for malicious scripts and viruses.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications (CC7.1.2). Self-assessment scans performed prior to every release or yearly.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party. Results reviewed by IT management. High/critical findings remediated in timely manner and added to risk register.

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to facilities: AWS responsible for data center physical security, not in scope
A1.2 - Environmental protections: Talentify has no office/working space, managed by building management/AWS
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2: Branch protection rules require merge request approval before production deployment
CC8.1.3: Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed to author; MFA required for SCM access
CC6.1.1: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production
CC6.6.1: Firewalls and intrusion detection tools configured on application gateway and production network
CC7.4.1: BCDR plan documented, reviewed annually, tested annually with different loss scenarios
CC6.1.3: Customer access appropriately segmented from other customer accounts (per-tenant segregation)
CC7.1.1: Infrastructure as code with configuration management tool for rollback capability
CC4.1.1: External penetration testing conducted annually by independent third-party

infrastructure.rationale: AWS with multi-AZ and apparent multi-region (VPC WEST/EAST) is solid, but the network diagram is explicitly a SAMPLE placeholder not their actual architecture. No specific regions named, no CDN, no serverless or advanced cloud services mentioned. Infrastructure as code is implied but tool not named.

application_architecture.rationale: Almost no application architecture details disclosed. No languages, frameworks, or specific compute services named. Pattern (monolith/microservices) not described. Branch protection and code review are confirmed, which is positive, but the lack of any technical specifics is concerning.

data_layer.rationale: Databases mentioned generically only. No specific database technologies, caching layers, or file storage services named. Backup policy exists but frequency not stated. Data classification and retention policies exist. Per-tenant segregation confirmed. Multi-AZ confirmed.

security_posture.rationale: Good coverage: MFA enforced, IDS/IPS via AWS, firewalls on application gateway, annual pen testing by third party, monthly vulnerability scans, antivirus/endpoint protection, content filtering via proxy, log protection, incident management process. No WAF explicitly named, no SIEM named, no specific security tools named by vendor.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection with merge request approval, IaC implied with configuration management and rollback capability, separate dev/test/prod environments, formal change management with emergency process. However, no CI/CD tool named, no source control platform named, no container orchestration mentioned.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing with different loss scenarios. Multi-AZ failover with ability to spin up across all AZs. Multi-region implied. However, no RTO/RPO disclosed, backup frequency not specified, and disaster recovery plan details are high-level.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Extremely heavy single-vendor dependency on AWS as sole cloud provider. No other specific technology vendors named in the entire report. Third-party services are described generically (endpoint security vendor, pen testing firm, certificate authority) but never identified by name.

overall.rationale: This report reads as a heavily templated SOC 2 Type 1 with significant boilerplate content and placeholder elements (sample network diagram, sample org chart, highlighted template instructions still visible, unsigned signing authority). While the control framework is reasonably comprehensive, the near-total absence of specific technology details makes meaningful technical due diligence very difficult. The report demonstrates basic security hygiene and governance but provides almost no insight into actual technical implementation.

red_flags: Network diagram is explicitly a SAMPLE PLACEHOLDER with yellow banner saying 'Please replace this with your actual network diagram' - this means the auditor accepted a template diagram, raising serious questions about audit rigor
Organization chart is also a SAMPLE PLACEHOLDER with instructions to replace it

Signing authority section on Management Assertion (page 10) has placeholder 'Your Name Here' with Signing Authority Name and Designation left blank/highlighted - the assertion may not have been properly signed

Multiple sections still contain highlighted yellow template instructions that were never deleted (e.g., 'Instructions: Modify this text to provide a brief background...', 'Instructions: Modify this text to provide more context...')
System description on page 12 says 'headquartered at [Company Address]' - a template placeholder that was never filled in

The report appears to describe Talentify as a 'compliance platform' but the original metadata describes it as a 'recruitment marketing agency' - significant identity mismatch suggests the system description may be boilerplate from the audit firm's template
Zero specific technology vendors named throughout entire 57-page report aside from AWS
No specific programming languages, frameworks, database systems, or monitoring tools identified anywhere
Audit firm (Accorp Partners) used license number PAC-FIRM-LIC-47383 - legitimacy should be verified
yellow_flags: SOC 2 Type 1 only (point-in-time design assessment, no operating effectiveness testing)
No RTO or RPO values disclosed despite having BCDR policy
No WAF mentioned despite being a web-based SaaS platform
No SIEM tool named despite having logging and alerting controls
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded as 'not relevant' - unusual for a compliance platform handling customer data
Heavy control reuse/repetition across multiple trust criteria (same controls mapped to many criteria, suggesting minimal actual control inventory)
Content filtering via proxy server is an outdated approach
green_flags: Unqualified opinion with zero exceptions noted
MFA enforced for production administrative access, source code management, and email
Branch protection with mandatory merge request approval before production deployment
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Infrastructure as code with rollback capability via configuration management tool
BCDR tested annually with different loss scenarios
Quarterly user access reviews including administrative privileges
Access revocation within one business day of termination
Vendor management policy with annual SOC report reviews and quarterly risk assessments for critical vendors without SOC reports
Formal incident management process with root cause analysis and lessons learned
Endpoint protection on all servers and workstations
key_observations: This is one of the most heavily templated SOC 2 reports encountered - the audit firm (Accorp Partners) appears to have provided a generic template that was minimally customized, with placeholder diagrams, instructions, and even an unsigned assertion left in the final deliverable

There is a fundamental identity confusion: the metadata and auditor scope describe 'a recruitment marketing agency that uses AI and expertise for better results' but the system description describes an AI-automated compliance platform - these are completely different businesses, suggesting the system description was copied from a template or the company pivoted without updating all documentation
The extremely generic nature of all technology references (no vendors, tools, or specific services named) makes it impossible to perform meaningful technical due diligence from this report alone
Despite the template issues, the control framework covers expected areas and the controls described are reasonable if actually implemented

The carve-out method for AWS means AWS's own controls are excluded - for a company 100% dependent on AWS with no physical infrastructure, this leaves a significant portion of the actual security posture unexamined in this report
